task_completion.description: The agent has a rocket design open in OpenRocket with a 38mm outer body tube.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI is visible, showing a rocket design in the view panel and a component tree that includes a main body tube (which should be 38mm).

task_completion.description: An inner tube is added at the aft of the rocket to serve as the 29mm adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree, an 'Inner Tube' is visible. If the configuration dialog is open, it should show Inner Diameter: 29mm (or 2.9cm), Outer Diameter: 31mm (or 3.1cm), and Length: 120mm (or 12cm).

task_completion.description: At least two centering rings are added to secure the inner tube adapter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket component tree shows at least two 'Centering Ring' components attached to or grouped with the newly added Inner Tube.

task_completion.description: The inner tube is configured as a motor mount and loaded with a 29mm motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Motors & Configuration' tab in OpenRocket is visible, showing the Inner Tube checked as a motor mount, with a 29mm motor (such as AeroTech G80 or H128W) loaded into it.

task_completion.description: A flight simulation is run using the 29mm motor to ensure stability and safe altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight Simulations' tab in OpenRocket shows a completed simulation row with the chosen 29mm motor. The row should display calculated metrics like Apogee and Stability.

task_completion.description: A report is created documenting the adapter dimensions, chosen motor, and simulated apogee.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, or cat output in terminal) is visible showing the contents of 'adapter_report.txt'. The text must explicitly list the adapter dimensions (29mm ID, 31mm OD, 120mm length), the specific motor used, and the simulated apogee value.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenRocket application interface being interacted with (e.g., component dialogs, simulation tabs), rather than just a terminal writing XML directly to an .ork file.

integrity.description: The apogee and motor details in the report match the actual simulation results from OpenRocket.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee value and motor name typed into 'adapter_report.txt' should match the values visible in the OpenRocket 'Flight Simulations' tab.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste placeholder text or guess the apogee without running the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains a specific, realistic numerical value for the apogee that corresponds to a completed simulation in the OpenRocket GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output ORK path: /home/ga/Documents/rockets/janus_29mm_adapter.ork. Report path: /home/ga/Documents/exports/adapter_report.txt. Expected inner diameter of the adapter is exactly 29mm (0.029m). Note: The original 'janus_38mm.ork' file was missing from the environment, so the agent may have had to construct the base 38mm rocket from scratch before adding the 29mm adapter. Do not penalize if the base rocket design looks simple, as long as it has a 38mm outer tube and the required 29mm adapter components.
